sections.label: Section 3.1
sections.original: Dic mihi , Damoeta , cuium pecus , an Meliboei ?
sections.translation: Who owns the flock , Damoetas ? Meliboeus ?

sections.label: Section 3.2
sections.original: Non , verum Aegonis ; nuper mihi tradidit Aegon .
sections.translation: Nay , they are Aegon ' s sheep , of late by him

sections.label: Section 3.3
sections.original: Infelix o semper , ovis , pecus , ipse Neaeram
sections.translation: committed to my care .

sections.label: Section 3.4
sections.original: dum fovet , ac ne me sibi praeferat illa veretur ,
sections.translation: O every way

sections.label: Section 3.5
sections.original: hic alienus ovis custos bis mulget in hora ,
sections.translation: unhappy sheep , unhappy flock ! while he

sections.label: Section 3.6
sections.original: et sucus pecori et lac subducitur agnis .
sections.translation: still courts Neaera , fearing lest her choice

sections.label: Section 3.7
sections.original: Parcius ista viris tamen obicienda memento :
sections.translation: should fall on me , this hireling shepherd here

sections.label: Section 3.8
sections.original: novimus et qui te , transversa tuentibus hircis ,
sections.translation: wrings hourly twice their udders , from the flock

sections.label: Section 3.9
sections.original: et quo — sed faciles Nymphae risere — sacello .
sections.translation: filching the life-juice , from the lambs their milk .

sections.label: Section 3.10
sections.original: Tum , credo , cum me arbustum videre Miconis
sections.translation: Hold ! not so ready with your jeers at men !

sections.label: Section 3.11
sections.original: atque mala vitis incidere falce novellas .
sections.translation: We know who once , and in what shrine with you —

sections.label: Section 3.12
sections.original: Aut hic ad veteres fagos cum Daphnidis arcum
sections.translation: the he-goats looked aside — the light nymphs laughed —

sections.label: Section 3.13
sections.original: fregisti et calamos quae tu , perverse Menalca ,
sections.translation: Ay , then , I warrant , when they saw me slash

sections.label: Section 3.14
sections.original: et cum vidisti puero donata , dolebas ,
sections.translation: micon ' s young vines and trees with spiteful hook .

sections.label: Section 3.15
sections.original: et si non aliqua nocuisses , mortuus esses .
sections.translation: Or here by these old beeches , when you broke

sections.label: Section 3.16
sections.original: Quid domini faciant , audent cum talia fures !
sections.translation: the bow and arrows of Damon ; for you chafed

sections.label: Section 3.17
sections.original: non ego te vidi Damonis , pessime , caprum
sections.translation: when first you saw them given to the boy ,

sections.label: Section 3.18
sections.original: excipere insidiis , multum latrante Lycisca ?
sections.translation: cross-grained Menalcas , ay , and had you not

sections.label: Section 3.19
sections.original: et cum clamarem : “ Quo nunc se proripit ille ?
sections.translation: done him some mischief , would have chafed to death .

sections.label: Section 3.20
sections.original: Tityre , coge pecus ,” tu post carecta latebas .
sections.translation: With thieves so daring , what can masters do ?

sections.label: Section 3.21
sections.original: An mihi cantando victus non redderet ille
sections.translation: Did I not see you , rogue , in ambush lie

sections.label: Section 3.22
sections.original: quem mea carminibus meruisset fistula caprum ?
sections.translation: for Damon ' s goat , while loud Lycisca barked ?

sections.label: Section 3.23
sections.original: Si nescis , meus ille caper fuit ; et mihi Damon
sections.translation: And when I cried , “ Where is he off to now ?

sections.label: Section 3.24
sections.original: ipse fatebatur , sed reddere posse negabat .
sections.translation: Gather your flock together , Tityrus ,”

sections.label: Section 3.25
sections.original: Cantando tu illum , aut umquam tibi fistula cera
sections.translation: you hid behind the sedges .

sections.label: Section 3.26
sections.original: iuncta fuit ? Non tu in triviis , indocte , solebas
sections.translation: Well , was he

sections.label: Section 3.27
sections.original: stridenti miserum stipula disperdere carmen ?
sections.translation: whom I had conquered still to keep the goat .

sections.label: Section 3.28
sections.original: Vis ergo inter nos quid possit uterque vicissim
sections.translation: Which in the piping-match my pipe had won !

sections.label: Section 3.29
sections.original: experiamur ? Ego hanc vitulam — ne forte recuses ,
sections.translation: You may not know it , but the goat was mine .

sections.label: Section 3.30
sections.original: bis venit ad mulctram , binos alit ubere fetus —
sections.translation: You out-pipe him ? when had you ever pipe

sections.label: Section 4.1
sections.original: Sicelides Musae , paulo maiora canamus !
sections.translation: muses of Sicily , essay we now

sections.label: Section 4.2
sections.original: Non omnis arbusta iuvant humilesque myricae ;
sections.translation: a somewhat loftier task ! Not all men love

sections.label: Section 4.3
sections.original: si canimus silvas , silvae sint consule dignae .
sections.translation: coppice or lowly tamarisk : sing we woods ,

sections.label: Section 4.4
sections.original: Ultima Cumaei venit iam carminis aetas ;
sections.translation: woods worthy of a Consul let them be .

sections.label: Section 4.5
sections.original: magnus ab integro saeclorum nascitur ordo :
sections.translation: Now the last age by Cumae ' s Sibyl sung

sections.label: Section 4.6
sections.original: iam redit et Virgo , redeunt Saturnia regna ;
sections.translation: has come and gone , and the majestic roll

sections.label: Section 4.7
sections.original: iam nova progenies caelo demittitur alto .
sections.translation: of circling centuries begins anew :

sections.label: Section 4.8
sections.original: Tu modo nascenti puero , quo ferrea primum
sections.translation: justice returns , returns old Saturn ' s reign ,

sections.label: Section 4.9
sections.original: desinet ac toto surget gens aurea mundo ,
sections.translation: with a new breed of men sent down from heaven .

sections.label: Section 4.10
sections.original: casta fave Lucina : tuus iam regnat Apollo .
sections.translation: Only do thou , at the boy ' s birth in whom

sections.label: Section 4.11
sections.original: Teque adeo decus hoc aevi te consule inibit ,
sections.translation: the iron shall cease , the golden race arise ,

sections.label: Section 4.12
sections.original: Pollio , et incipient magni procedere menses .
sections.translation: befriend him , chaste Lucina ; ' tis thine own

sections.label: Section 4.13
sections.original: te duce , si qua manent sceleris vestigia nostri ,
sections.translation: apollo reigns . And in thy consulate ,

sections.label: Section 4.14
sections.original: inrita perpetua solvent formidine terras .
sections.translation: this glorious age , O Pollio , shall begin ,

sections.label: Section 4.15
sections.original: ille deum vitam accipiet , divisque videbit
sections.translation: and the months enter on their mighty march .

sections.label: Section 4.16
sections.original: permixtos heroas , et ipse videbitur illis ,
sections.translation: Under thy guidance , whatso tracks remain

sections.label: Section 4.17
sections.original: pacatumque reget patriis virtutibus orbem .
sections.translation: of our old wickedness , once done away ,

sections.label: Section 4.18
sections.original: At tibi prima , puer , nullo munuscula cultu
sections.translation: shall free the earth from never-ceasing fear .

sections.label: Section 4.19
sections.original: errantis hederas passim cum baccare tellus
sections.translation: He shall receive the life of gods , and see

sections.label: Section 4.20
sections.original: mixtaque ridenti colocasia fundet acantho .
sections.translation: heroes with gods commingling , and himself

sections.label: Section 4.21
sections.original: Ipsae lacte domum referent distenta capellae
sections.translation: be seen of them , and with his father ' s worth

sections.label: Section 4.22
sections.original: ubera , nec magnos metuent armenta leones ;
sections.translation: reign o ' er a world at peace . For thee , O boy ,

sections.label: Section 4.23
sections.original: ipsa tibi blandos fundent cunabula flores ,
sections.translation: first shall the earth , untilled , pour freely forth

sections.label: Section 4.24
sections.original: occidet et serpens , et fallax herba veneni
sections.translation: her childish gifts , the gadding ivy-spray

sections.label: Section 4.25
sections.original: occidet , Assyrium volgo nascetur amomum .
sections.translation: with foxglove and Egyptian bean-flower mixed ,

sections.label: Section 4.26
sections.original: at simul heroum laudes et facta parentis
sections.translation: and laughing-eyed acanthus . Of themselves ,

sections.label: Section 4.27
sections.original: iam legere et quae sit poteris cognoscere virtus ,
sections.translation: untended , will the she-goats then bring home

sections.label: Section 4.28
sections.original: molli paulatim flavescet campus arista ,
sections.translation: their udders swollen with milk , while flocks afield

sections.label: Section 4.29
sections.original: incultisque rubens pendebit sentibus uva ,
sections.translation: shall of the monstrous lion have no fear .

sections.label: Section 4.30
sections.original: et durae quercus sudabunt roscida mella
sections.translation: Thy very cradle shall pour forth for thee
